English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Wason Collection records
Cornell University. Library. Wason Collection
13-6-1721
6.4 cubic feet. 6.4 cubic feet.
1918-1988. Correspondence, articles, clippings, reports, notebooks, and articles. Mostly department correspondence, largely that of Gussie Esther Gaskill, first curator of the Wason Collection (1927-1963); materials concerning trips to China and Japan; projects; bibliographies; acquisitions, cataloging, and other records; accessions list; accounting statistics, and general office files, 1976-1985; annual reports of the East Asian Librarian, 1972-1988. Correspondence relating to Charles Wason's bequest, especially correspondence between Mrs. Charles Wason and the Arthur H. Clark Company, correspondence between Mrs. Wason and Gussie Gaskill, drawing and photographs of the library at the Wason home, and articles, reports and clippings about the collection. Three handwritten notebooks by T.F. Crane listing the Wason Collection, ca. 1922. Collection material in English <<<<<<< HEAD 1 ======= 1 >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab 2072250
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu
EAD encoding:
Martin Heggestad, November 2002
updated:
RMC Staff, April 2011
ORGANIZATIONAL HISTORY
The Wason Collection was bequeathed to Cornell University in 1918 by Charles William Wason. It began as a collection of English-language works on China and the Chinese. Its scope and size have since been greatly enlarged, and it is now the only major East Asian collection in the country to include all Western language materials in the areas covered in a special collection together with the materials in the languages of those areas.
Cite As:
Cornell University Wason Collection records, #13-6-1721. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Scope and content
Mostly department correspondence, largely that of Gussie Esther Gaskill, first curator of the Wason Collection (1927-1963); materials concerning trips to China and Japan; projects; bibliographies; acquisitions, cataloging, and other records; accessions list; accounting statistics, and general office files, 1976-1985; annual reports of the East Asian Librarian, 1972-1988. Correspondence relating to Charles Wason's bequest, especially correspondence between Mrs. Charles Wason and the Arthur H. Clark Company, correspondence between Mrs. Wason and Gussie Gaskill, drawing and photographs of the library at the Wason home, and articles, reports and clippings about the collection. Three handwritten notebooks by T.F. Crane listing the Wason Collection, ca. 1922.
Scope and content
Files from Thomas Hahn, Wason Curator until 2006, include materials about the Encyclopedia Maxima, Hu Shih, ships' logs, a portrait of Mao by Andy Warhol, other acquisitions, and exhibitions.
Scope and content
Miscellaneous items relating to the Wason Collection include letters, description, and notes about the Ming Treasure Note, 1934; and letter about and translation of a Boxer Rebellion poster, 1921.
Access Restrictions:
Access to Boxes 2-4 restricted to the permission of the office of origin.
East Asia. Japan. China. Women librarians. Librarians. Libraries -- Special collections -- Chinese literature. Gaskill, Gussie Esther, 1898- Wason, Charles William, 1854-1918. Wason, Charles William, Mrs. Crane, Thomas Frederick, 1844-1927. Cornell University -- : Library.

Scope and Contents
